steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content: The passage sets up a clear progression: when few minor planets had been discovered, the naming task was [blank], but now that over 500,000 exist, the task has become nearly impossible. The blank must describe what the task was when it was still being accomplished.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Feasible means achievable. When few minor planets had been discovered, naming was feasible; as the number grew to over 500,000, the task became nearly impossible.

The other choices either don't address feasibility, describe the wrong aspect of the problem, or contradict the passage's suggestion that naming was achievable in the past.

That confirms B is correct.
